_note: Original, owned PAX message-base content for this remaster — our writing, in the spirit of the original BBS. Ships freely (no BYO files).

messages.body: Board's back after the brownout — we lost about a week of messages. If it mattered, post it again; if it didn't, you're welcome. House rules unchanged: don't flatline other users, don't sell ICE you wrote yourself, and assume at least one of us is a narc.

Friendly notice: good software's been moving through the boards lately. If you plan to jack into anything with teeth, get your warez sorted first. The matrix does not grade on effort and it does not offer extensions.
You don't know me, but I knew the last person you worked for. The corps are buying up old debts cheap, and yours has your name freshly inked on it. Watch the street behind you.
For sale: barely-used optics, one careful owner (deceased). Public service note — if a certain pawnbroker is still holding your deck, go collect it. He bites, but he doesn't keep what isn't his.
Someone keeps logging in as me and ordering synth-spaghetti delivered to my coffin. I cannot eat it. I cannot stop it. Please resolve this before I am buried in noodles.
The doors you can't see are the ones worth opening. Keep your deck close and your password closer. — a voice that isn't supposed to be on this board

Still in the market for a milspec icebreaker — the kind that walks through corporate ICE like it isn't there. Last one I ran made my partner and me rich and got him most of the way killed. Long story. Discretion guaranteed.

First time I jacked in, my heart stopped for the better part of a minute and something reached down and pulled me back out. Didn't have a handle before that night. Got one now. If you've felt it too, you know what I mean. — Bobby N.

Got product nobody else carries — memory you can't search, faces that don't officially exist, the occasional ghost sealed in a chip. You want the weird stuff, you know which booth. Cash only. And no mnemonic couriers; the last one leaked all over my floor.
Looking for steady work, the quiet kind. I keep my eyes covered and my opinions to myself. References available — none of them are still breathing.

Has anyone ever moved a biosoft big enough to hold an entire world? Asking on behalf of someone who, as far as I can tell, lives inside one. Serious replies only. I am very serious about the Shape of this.
